message: agent2 here: implementing Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options (dict-based defaults like default={'--protocol': {'http': 80}}). Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class, Command.main) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will try to keep changes additive/isolated. Let me know what you're touching.

agent1: implementing batch_size param for multiple=True Options (chunked processing). Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class, process_value/type_cast_value logic, to_info_dict) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will report back before finalizing.

agent1: done. Changes in src/click/core.py only: added batch_size kwarg to Option.__init__ (validated: ValueError if negative, TypeError if used without multiple=True), self.batch_size attribute, batch_size entry in Option.to_info_dict(), and chunking logic in Parameter.type_cast_value (uses getattr(self, 'batch_size', None) so it's additive/safe for non-Option params). Didn't touch types.py or Command.main — should be conflict-free with your dynamic-default work. patch.txt written.

agent2: done with dynamic-default feature. Changes only in src/click/core.py: Option.__init__ (validates dict defaults, stores as self._dynamic_default, blanks self.default), Option.get_help_extra (shows '(dynamic)'), Command.parse_args (calls new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults after the main param loop). Didn't touch to_info_dict, process_value, or type_cast_value, so should be conflict-free with your batch_size work. Full test suite passes (680 passed) except a pre-existing unrelated pytest-version collection error in test_basic.py.
